checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully reflects the headings and list structures found in the source PDF. There are no invented paragraphs, sentences, or section labels that do not have a basis in the original document. The metadata title is auto-generated, which is an allowable change.

The output JSON does not contain any fabricated contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or URLs. While two URLs from the source are missing in the output, no new or incorrect ones have been added.
All 13 pages of the source PDF are represented in the output JSON either via their primary headings or the associated image elements. No entire page or major section is omitted.
The source document does not contain any data tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output document has significant text omissions throughout. On Page 3 (Outline), the nested items under 'Accomplishments' and the final item 'Future Priorities' are missing. On Page 8, 9, 11, and 12, the primary descriptive text labels for the icons (e.g., 'ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE', 'NC ONEMAP CLOUD MIGRATION', etc.) are entirely absent. Additionally, the URLs on Page 2 and Page 13 are missing. These omissions constitute more than 25% of the text for those specific sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3: Nested list items under Accomplishments (Data Governance, Public Access to GIS Data, Collaboration for Land Information, Intergovernmental Collaboration, Sharing Information and Knowledge) and final item 'Future Priorities'. Page 8: 'ADDRESS NC STEERING COMMITTEE', 'WORKING GROUP FOR SEAMLESS PARCELS'. Page 9: 'NC ONEMAP CLOUD MIGRATION', 'TAC INFRASTRUCTURE WORKING GROUP'. Page 11: '2020 CENSUS UPDATES AND ASSISTANCE', 'NOAA LAND COVER PILOT', 'ELEVATION DATA', 'HYDROGRAPHY WORKING GROUP'. Page 12: 'ESRI SPECIAL ACHIEVEMENT AWARD', 'ESRI ENTERPRISE LICENSE AGREEMENT', 'PROFESSIONAL MEETINGS'. Page 13: 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/feedback'. Page 2: 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc-annual-report-FY-20-21'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text described in the source evidence is completely missing from the element lists in the corresponding sections of the JSON.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that is captured is mostly accurate. Minor reformatting such as the concatenation of titles and names on the first page ('James Weaver State Chief Information Officer...') is considered acceptable normalization for accessibility.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output document and the source PDF. No text blocks or paragraphs from the source PDF appear more than once in the structured JSON output. Each page's content is represented once in its corresponding section.

The heading hierarchy is consistent throughout the document. Most page titles are mapped to H1 (e.g., element 1, 25, 31), and secondary titles or subtitles are mapped to H2 (e.g., element 5, 84). There are no instances of skipped heading levels (like H1 directly to H3).
The source PDF does not contain any tables. Consequently, the JSON output contains no table elements, and this check is not applicable.

The reading order in the JSON output follows the page-by-page flow of the source PDF. Within pages, the order logically moves from top to bottom and left to right (as seen on the cover page where the left column precedes the right column).

The list on Page 3 (Outline) is incorrectly converted. The JSON output (element 26) truncates the list, omitting the sub-items under 'Accomplishments' and the final item 'Future Priorities'. Furthermore, the nested structure indicated in the PDF is not reflected in the flat 5-item list provided in the JSON.
checks.b_evidence_source: The list on page 3 includes: Executive Summary, About the Council, FY 20-21 Highlights, Goals, Accomplishments (with 5 sub-items), and Future Priorities.

checks.a_analysis: The links present in the source PDF (on page 2 and page 13) are correctly captured in the JSON output. No fabricated links were discovered.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the overall structural organization of the source PDF. The sequence of sections (Title, What We're Doing, Outline, Highlights, Collaboration sections, Questions) matches the original document. Although icons and some images are missing, the hierarchical flow and relative positioning of the text content remain consistent with the source's intent for a single-column responsive HTML layout.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present in the HTML output.

Multiple meaningful images and icons from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the circular icons representing various programs and committees on pages 8 through 12 (e.g., the map pin for Address NC, the compass for Seamless Parcels, the cloud icon for NC OneMap migration, etc.) are entirely absent. Furthermore, the prominent photograph of people raising their hands on the final 'Questions?' page (PDF page 13) is missing from the corresponding section in the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 13 of the PDF shows a circular image of people in a presentation. Pages 8-12 show various circular icons for programs.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Questions?' section and collaboration sections (pages 8-12) in the HTML screenshot lack these images and icons.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: Collaboration for Data Governance, Collaboration for Public Access, Collaboration for Land Information, Intergovernmental Collaboration, Sharing Information, and Questions?.

checks.a_analysis: There is significant visual duplication of content in the HTML output. In the 'What We're Doing' section, the text contained within the graphical boxes (e.g., 'Council Meetings Meeting Schedule...') is repeated as plain text paragraphs immediately below the boxes. Additionally, the NCDIT logo is duplicated multiple times, appearing in pairs at the start of several sections (e.g., before 'What We're Doing' and 'Outline'), which is not reflective of the source document's design.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 of the PDF shows text only once inside styled boxes. The NCDIT logo appears once in the footer area.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the box text repeated as plain text below the boxes. Multiple NCDIT logos are stacked at the start of sections.
